course.title: Memory Management and Memory Safety in Modern C23
course.description: Learners who can reason about data structures and algorithms but are new to C and need to design, test, diagnose, optimize, and defend explicit memory and resource lifetimes from first principles through production systems. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate c23 foundations needed for memory work; translation, types, conversions, objects, lifetime, representation, alignment, arrays, pointers, strings, structures, unions, callbacks, automatic storage, heap allocation, size arithmetic, ownership, cleanup, realloc, temporal and spatial defects, initialization, undefined behavior, aliasing, provenance, arenas, pools, reference counting, tracing collection, external resources, concurrency, abi, ffi, webassembly linear memory, sanitizers, static analysis, fuzzing, profiling, hardening, testing, release, rollback, and incident defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate more than two hundred exact runnable changed-case c programs in isolated browser practice, plus explicit c23, gcc 16, clang 22, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, abi, thread, python, rust, webassembly, load, fault, release, and production transfer evidence through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative stakeholder projects and a performance-based memory-safety certification defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves earlier size, bounds, lifetime, ownership, cleanup, accessibility, security, testing, and evidence habits before adding one bounded memory concept.
Every coding task produces changed-case output and an inspectable invariant. Code shape alone, a clean compile, one passing browser run, or one sanitizer run is never sufficient mastery evidence.

Browser code stays inside the PicoC subset and disposable worker. C23 syntax and semantics, optimizing compilers, native libraries, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production claims name an explicit controlled transfer gate.
Unsafe examples are presented as defects to predict, diagnose, repair, and regression-test; they are never copied into starter patterns as acceptable production code.

competencies.statement: Apply ISO/IEC TS 6010 provenance concepts as reviewed guidance without mislabeling them as universally implemented C23 behavior.
misconceptions: The provenance technical specification is already identical to every production compiler memory model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply ISO/IEC TS 6010 provenance concepts as reviewed guidance without mislabeling them as universally implemented C23 behavior.

competencies.statement: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.

competencies.statement: Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.
misconceptions: Arena reset is equivalent to individually running every object destructor.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.

competencies.statement: Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.
misconceptions: A singly linked free list needs no corruption checks because nodes are fixed size.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.

competencies.statement: Choose size classes, alignment, page ownership, and internal-fragmentation metrics from workload evidence.
misconceptions: One size class minimizes both waste and allocator overhead.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose size classes, alignment, page ownership, and internal-fragmentation metrics from workload evidence.

competencies.statement: Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.
misconceptions: RSS alone identifies allocator fragmentation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.

competencies.statement: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.
misconceptions: Every pointer copy should increment a reference count.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.

competencies.statement: Prevent count overflow, underflow, resurrection, and release reentrancy with explicit states.
misconceptions: Reference counts cannot overflow in realistic programs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent count overflow, underflow, resurrection, and release reentrancy with explicit states.

competencies.statement: Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.
misconceptions: A reference count reaching zero eventually is guaranteed if the program stops using an object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.

competencies.statement: Validate weak handles against liveness and generation before acquiring a new strong reference.
misconceptions: A weak pointer can be safely inspected after the object is destroyed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate weak handles against liveness and generation before acquiring a new strong reference.

competencies.statement: Evaluate atomicity, contention, batching, deferred release, and memory-order requirements for shared counts.
misconceptions: Making the count atomic makes the whole referenced object thread-safe.

competencies.statement: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.
misconceptions: An unreachable object is necessarily unused and safe to finalize immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.

competencies.statement: Mark reachable objects, sweep only unmarked allocations, and reset metadata without losing new or mutated edges.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Mark reachable objects, sweep only unmarked allocations, and reset metadata without losing new or mutated edges.

competencies.statement: Explain false retention and invalid reclamation risks when arbitrary words may be treated as pointers.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain false retention and invalid reclamation risks when arbitrary words may be treated as pointers.

competencies.statement: Constrain finalizer order, reentrancy, resurrection, external resources, and repeated collection behavior.
misconceptions: A garbage collector provides deterministic file and lock release.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain finalizer order, reentrancy, resurrection, external resources, and repeated collection behavior.

competencies.statement: Relate generational and concurrent collection to write barriers, mutator cooperation, pause, throughput, and memory tradeoffs.

competencies.statement: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.

competencies.statement: Pair acquisition with one idempotent or guarded release operation and a valid empty state.

competencies.statement: Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.

competencies.statement: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.

competencies.statement: Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.
misconceptions: A release store alone prevents an object from being freed too soon.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.

competencies.statement: Compare locks, reference counts, hazard pointers, epochs, RCU-like schemes, and ownership transfer for safe reclamation.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare locks, reference counts, hazard pointers, epochs, RCU-like schemes, and ownership transfer for safe reclamation.

competencies.statement: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.
misconceptions: A shared C header guarantees binary compatibility across all targets.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.

competencies.statement: Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.

competencies.statement: Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.
misconceptions: A pointer and null terminator fully describe every foreign buffer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.

competencies.statement: Convert errors to stable status and out-value contracts and prevent language exceptions or panics from crossing unsupported C frames.
misconceptions: Rust panic or Python exception propagation through C is automatically ABI-safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Convert errors to stable status and out-value contracts and prevent language exceptions or panics from crossing unsupported C frames.

competencies.statement: Treat WebAssembly pointers as offsets into bounded linear memory with copy, growth, lifetime, and host-reference rules.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat WebAssembly pointers as offsets into bounded linear memory with copy, growth, lifetime, and host-reference rules.

misconceptions: ASan detects uninitialized reads, every leak, and every temporal defect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.

competencies.statement: Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer and fully instrumented MemorySanitizer environments for their distinct defect classes.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer and fully instrumented MemorySanitizer environments for their distinct defect classes.

competencies.statement: Run Clang and GCC static analyzers, preserve paths and assumptions, classify findings, and regression-test repairs.
misconceptions: Static analyzer warnings are either proofs or ignorable false positives.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run Clang and GCC static analyzers, preserve paths and assumptions, classify findings, and regression-test repairs.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build a deterministic libFuzzer target with bounded input, sanitizer instrumentation, corpus, dictionary, timeout, and minimized regressions.

competencies.statement: Maintain a defect-by-tool and platform matrix so unsupported configurations and blind spots remain explicit.
misconceptions: Running every tool once is better than selecting compatible complementary gates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a defect-by-tool and platform matrix so unsupported configurations and blind spots remain explicit.

competencies.statement: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.
misconceptions: The number of malloc calls alone explains memory performance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.

competencies.statement: Relate object layout, traversal order, working set, cache behavior, false sharing, and measured latency.

competencies.statement: Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.
misconceptions: A benchmark winner on one micro-test is the best allocator for production.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.

competencies.statement: Evaluate allocator checks, guard pages, canaries, randomization, control-flow defenses, fortification, and production cost.
misconceptions: Hardening flags repair undefined behavior in source code.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate allocator checks, guard pages, canaries, randomization, control-flow defenses, fortification, and production cost.

competencies.statement: Require functional, sanitizer, resource, load, and changed-workload evidence before accepting a memory optimization.

credential.title: Memory Management and Memory Safety in Modern C23 Certificate

modules.title: C Memory Outcomes, Evidence, and Toolchain Boundaries
modules.description: A civic sensor team must distinguish a passing browser model from evidence that its native C release is safe on supported targets. Learners cumulatively produce memory-safety evidence and transfer matrix through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.
Pin ISO/IEC 9899:2024, reviewed implementation support, diagnostics, extensions, and target ABI as a compatibility contract.
Trace source, preprocessing, translation, linking, loading, execution, and observable output without collapsing their failure layers.

modules.title: Translation Units, Declarations, Diagnostics, and Linking
modules.description: A portable telemetry library compiles in one file but fails or silently changes behavior after its API is split across files. Learners cumulatively produce warning-clean multi-file interface dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Predict macro expansion, conditional compilation, include guards, and header ownership before translation.
Distinguish declarations, tentative and full definitions, linkage, compatible types, and one-definition responsibilities.
Use complete prototypes and compatible parameter and return types across every caller boundary.

modules.title: C Values, Integer Conversions, and Size Arithmetic
modules.description: A binary record parser receives attacker-controlled counts that cross signedness and multiplication boundaries before allocation. Learners cumulatively produce checked size and conversion policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Select integer types from range, signedness, rank, width, and representation requirements rather than familiar spelling.
Trace integer promotions and usual arithmetic conversions before comparing, shifting, indexing, or sizing.
Use size_t for object sizes, ptrdiff_t for valid pointer differences, and guarded conversions at API boundaries.

modules.title: Objects, Scope, Linkage, Storage Duration, and Lifetime
modules.description: An embedded controller caches addresses across callbacks while automatic, static, thread, and allocated objects begin and end at different times. Learners cumulatively produce object lifetime and address ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.
Map scope, linkage, and storage duration independently for block, file, static, thread, and allocated objects.
Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.

modules.title: Bytes, Object Representation, Alignment, Padding, and Endianness
modules.description: A device protocol works on one architecture but leaks padding and faults on a stricter-alignment target. Learners cumulatively produce portable binary representation codec through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.
Compute and satisfy fundamental, extended, member, and allocated alignment requirements before access.
Measure rather than assume member offsets, tail padding, and layout changes across ABI targets.

modules.title: Arrays, Pointers, Arithmetic, and Bounds
modules.description: A raster pipeline slices rows and subviews while changed dimensions expose one-past, negative-offset, and lost-bound defects. Learners cumulatively produce bounded image-slice library through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Preserve array extent where possible and predict array-to-pointer conversion at expressions and parameters.
Perform pointer arithmetic only within one array object or one-past it and avoid dereferencing one-past pointers.
State and check lower and upper index invariants before access, including zero-length and changed-shape cases.

modules.title: Strings, Byte Buffers, Length, Capacity, and Encoding
modules.description: An accessible terminal application receives filenames, UTF-8 labels, and binary payloads through APIs that disagree about termination and length. Learners cumulatively produce length-aware text and byte boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish C strings, unterminated character arrays, byte buffers, string literals, and encoded text.
Track logical length, allocated capacity, required terminator space, and maximum scan distance separately.
Use size-aware copying and formatting with return-value checks and truncation policy.

modules.title: Structures, Unions, Tagged Variants, and Flexible Arrays
modules.description: A packet queue combines headers and payloads while a changed variant exposes padding, inactive-union, and size-formula errors. Learners cumulatively produce tagged packet representation through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Initialize, copy, compare, and validate structures by members and invariants rather than raw-byte assumptions.
Pair a union with an explicit discriminant and access only the active, validated member.
Allocate flexible-array objects with checked header-plus-elements arithmetic and record their element count.

modules.title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces
modules.description: A plugin registry invokes callbacks after their contexts change ownership and one implementation uses an incompatible signature. Learners cumulatively produce typed callback and context registry through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.

modules.title: Automatic Storage, Recursion, VLAs, and Escaping Addresses
modules.description: A recursive document walker succeeds in tests but overflows a constrained worker and returns pointers into expired frames. Learners cumulatively produce bounded stack-use redesign through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep pointers to automatic objects within the execution and subobject lifetimes that make them valid.
Bound recursion by adversarial input depth, frame cost, and target stack limits or replace it with explicit storage.
Use variable-length arrays only under explicit positive bounds and stack budgets, with portable alternatives.

modules.title: Heap Allocation Contracts and Failure Handling
modules.description: A batch importer assumes allocation succeeds and treats zero-byte requests differently across supported C libraries. Learners cumulatively produce allocation wrapper with explicit outcomes through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Apply malloc and calloc size, initialization, alignment, zero-size, and failure contracts without dereferencing assumptions.
Propagate allocation failure without partial-state leaks, null dereferences, or impossible-error assumptions.
Choose and test a project policy for zero elements rather than depending on an implementation-specific returned pointer.

modules.title: Allocation Formulas, Overflow, and Capacity Proofs
modules.description: A scientific grid grows from untrusted dimensions and allocates too little after an apparently reasonable multiplication wraps. Learners cumulatively produce overflow-safe grid allocator through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Prove count times element-size representability before calling allocation functions.
Check header, padding, alignment, terminator, and payload additions in a safe order.
Characterize whether the target calloc detects product overflow while retaining caller-side policy and tests.

modules.title: Ownership, Borrowing, Aliasing, and API Contracts
modules.description: A media index passes buffers through parsers, caches, and callbacks where no component knows who must release or may mutate them. Learners cumulatively produce ownership-annotated buffer API through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify each pointer as unique owner, shared owner, mutable borrow, immutable borrow, weak observation, or non-owning handle.
Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.
Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.

modules.title: Cleanup, Partial Initialization, and Error Paths
modules.description: A configuration loader acquires buffers, files, locks, and parsed objects before failures at each stage. Learners cumulatively produce failure-atomic resource constructor through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.
Release acquired resources in dependency-safe reverse order with idempotent or guarded cleanup.
Use structured goto cleanup when it centralizes unwinding without skipping initialization or obscuring ownership.

modules.title: realloc Semantics and Transactional Growth
modules.description: A dynamic event list grows under memory pressure and loses its only pointer when resizing fails. Learners cumulatively produce amortized vector with failure invariants through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Store realloc results transactionally so allocation failure preserves the original live block.
Invalidate or rebase all interior pointers and aliases when realloc may move storage.
Choose bounded geometric growth from amortized cost, overflow, maximum capacity, and memory-pressure evidence.

modules.title: Use-After-Free, Double Free, and Stale Aliases
modules.description: An asynchronous job queue keeps aliases after cancellation and occasionally corrupts a newly allocated job at the same address. Learners cumulatively produce generation-checked handle repair through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.
Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.

modules.title: Buffer Overflow, Underflow, and Copy Boundaries
modules.description: A network frame rewriter moves overlapping regions and an off-by-one defect writes before or beyond the packet buffer. Learners cumulatively produce bounded frame transformation through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.
Validate both readable source and writable destination extents before memcpy-like operations.
Choose memcpy only for proven non-overlap and memmove for overlapping byte ranges.

modules.title: Initialization, Indeterminate Values, and Undefined Behavior
modules.description: An optimized release makes an uninitialized-state bug disappear in debug and reappear with a different compiler. Learners cumulatively produce defined-state initialization audit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.
Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.
Explain how undefined behavior removes semantic constraints and invalidates debug-build intuition.

modules.title: Aliasing, Effective Type, Provenance, and Type Punning
modules.description: A high-performance decoder casts byte storage into structures and changes output only under optimization. Learners cumulatively produce defined binary decoder with provenance notes through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Access allocated and copied storage through types permitted by effective-type rules or defined character-byte paths.
Preserve optimizer-visible non-aliasing rules and test any deliberate compiler-extension policy.
Use memcpy into a live destination object for representation transfer and validate the resulting value domain.

modules.title: Arenas, Pools, Slabs, and Fragmentation
modules.description: A game simulation creates millions of short-lived entities and must trade individual release for bounded phase ownership. Learners cumulatively produce instrumented arena and object pool through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.
Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.
Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.

modules.title: Reference Counting, Weak References, and Cycles
modules.description: A document graph shares nodes across views and leaks after parent-child cycles appear. Learners cumulatively produce overflow-safe reference-counted graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.
Prevent count overflow, underflow, resurrection, and release reentrancy with explicit states.
Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.

modules.title: Tracing Garbage Collection and Conservative Limits
modules.description: A language runtime must reclaim cyclic object graphs while preserving roots held by stacks, globals, and foreign code. Learners cumulatively produce small mark-and-sweep collector model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.
Mark reachable objects, sweep only unmarked allocations, and reset metadata without losing new or mutated edges.
Explain false retention and invalid reclamation risks when arbitrary words may be treated as pointers.

modules.title: Files, Descriptors, Locks, and RAII-Like C Patterns
modules.description: A service reload path owns heap buffers, FILE streams, descriptors, sockets, and mutexes across cancellation and errors. Learners cumulatively produce uniform resource-lifetime wrapper through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.
Pair acquisition with one idempotent or guarded release operation and a valid empty state.
Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.

modules.title: Threads, Atomics, Data Races, and Safe Reclamation
modules.description: A lock-free metrics cache removes nodes while readers still hold addresses and ordinary tests rarely reproduce the race. Learners cumulatively produce threaded lifetime and reclamation proof through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.
Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.
Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.

modules.title: ABI, Shared Libraries, and Foreign-Function Memory
modules.description: A C library shares opaque handles and buffers with Rust, Python, and WebAssembly consumers that use different allocators and unwind rules. Learners cumulatively produce versioned cross-language ownership ABI through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.
Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.
Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.

modules.title: Sanitizers, Static Analysis, and Fuzzing
modules.description: A security parser needs fast local feedback, nightly deep instrumentation, adversarial generation, and reproducible findings. Learners cumulatively produce layered memory-defect test matrix through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer and fully instrumented MemorySanitizer environments for their distinct defect classes.
Run Clang and GCC static analyzers, preserve paths and assumptions, classify findings, and regression-test repairs.

modules.title: Profiling, Allocator Performance, and Hardening
modules.description: A low-latency gateway reduces allocations but increases peak memory, fragmentation, and tail pauses under changed traffic. Learners cumulatively produce measured allocator optimization report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.
Relate object layout, traversal order, working set, cache behavior, false sharing, and measured latency.
Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.

modules.title: Testing, Release Gates, and Memory Incident Defense
modules.description: A critical native agent must ship a memory rewrite with compatibility, rollback, observability, and practiced incident recovery. Learners cumulatively produce production memory-safety defense dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, ABI, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk.
Test supported GCC and Clang versions, optimization modes, architectures, libraries, feature macros, and warning baselines.
Pin source, dependencies, compiler, flags, generated files, symbols, SBOM, provenance, and artifact identity.
